candidate_profile.narrative: M.S. CS student (Dec 2025, Univ. of Dayton) with a dual B.A. in Applied Math + CS from Augustana. Started with tutoring and IT help desk, moved into formal verification research (Rocq/Coq, IEEE KSE 2024 co-author), then pivoted to production systems: FastAPI platforms, Go networking, cloud infra on Proxmox/Terraform, and AI tooling with the Claude API. Currently contributing to GitLab open source and building biophysical simulation infrastructure at Carboncopies. The through-line is building real systems that work under real constraints — research-grade rigor applied to production-grade engineering.

portrays_well: AI/LLM integration — Claude API, LangChain, agent orchestration, MCP
Cloud and homelab infrastructure — Proxmox, Terraform, Ansible, Docker, k8s
Research credibility — IEEE publication, formal verification, RL adversarial robustness
iOS SwiftUI development — OutfitTracker, CalAI, SpotiSwipe
known_gaps: Limited enterprise Java/Spring or .NET production experience
No published iOS app on App Store yet (OutfitTracker in progress)
Homelab infra is personal scale — no hyperscaler (AWS/GCP/Azure) production on-call history
Go experience is project-level, not 2+ years professional
not_this: Do not pitch as a PM, TPM, or product role
Do not claim C++ or CUDA production expertise (Jetson project is academic)
Do not overstate ML research — KSE 2024 is 2nd author, Coq paper is draft not published
Do not pitch for defense/clearance roles or export-controlled positions
Do not claim AWS production on-call — homelab only

target_posture.auth_urgency: OPT active (authorized to work, no sponsorship needed now). STEM OPT extension available — up to 3 years total. No H-1B required yet. 'Authorized to work in the US' roles qualify.
constraints: Remote-first preferred; open to Harrisburg PA or relocation for strong offers
No US Citizen / GC only roles
No explicit 'no sponsorship' if OPT counts as no-sponsorship (flag ambiguous cases)

_meta.format: plain-text. Bullet formula: 'Built A doing B using C, which produced D — quantify D'. Buzzwords front-loaded. No professional summary. No new-grad language.
_meta.positioning: Software engineer with production deployments, open-source contributions, and research publications. Builds API platforms, AI workflows, and cloud infrastructure. FastAPI/Python, Go, TypeScript, Terraform.
_meta.usage: v2.3: use data.work=[{id,bullets[]}] in build scripts. Work IDs: gitlab|carboncopies|udayton|augustana. All bullets pre-validated at 116 chars max. Do NOT add bullets outside this file without running validate.py.
bullet_rules: Max 116 chars WITH spaces — hard limit, guaranteed 1-line at 11pt Calibri, Letter, 0.5in margins
Tagline max 76 chars WITH spaces — hard limit, 1-line at 12pt Calibri
Project header (name + ' | ' + stack + GitHub + date) max 116 chars — hard limit
Lead with varied non-repeated action verbs — no two bullets in one resume should start with the same verb
Lead with the most ATS-important keyword or tool
IMPACT IS MANDATORY: every bullet must answer 'so what?' — add before/after, time saved, % improvement, or business outcome. Activity-only bullets (no outcome) are disallowed.
BUSINESS CONTEXT: at least 1 bullet per work experience should explain WHY the work mattered — what did the team/user/business gain? Not just what was built, but the consequence.
ACTIVITY-ONLY RED FLAGS — always rewrite: 'Collaborated with...', 'Participated in...', 'Assisted with...', 'Worked on...' unless followed by a concrete quantified outcome.
Research bullets: state PURPOSE + ACHIEVEMENT + IMPACT — no fabrication
Do not fabricate stack items, metrics, or features not actually built
Bullets should tell a story a non-technical HM can understand
Use short_stack field for project header (3-4 most ATS-important techs, ≤40 chars)
tagline_rules: AVOID generic 'experienced in Tech1, Tech2, Tech3, Tech4' — it differentiates nothing
USE value-oriented format: '{Job Title} building {what} with {tech}' or '{Job Title} — {achievement/differentiator}'
GOOD: 'Software Engineer building REST APIs in Python and Go with cloud automation' (73c)
GOOD: 'Backend Engineer — 4+ prod features/sprint; FastAPI, distributed systems' (71c)
BAD: 'Software Engineer experienced in Python, REST APIs, Go, and Cloud Services' (generic, no proof)

contact.name: Quoc-Viet Bui
contact.work_auth: OPT + STEM OPT (up to 3 years, no H-1B sponsorship needed yet)

tagline_note: DO NOT use 'experienced in Tech1, Tech2, Tech3, Tech4' — that is generic and differentiates nothing. Use a VALUE-ORIENTED format: '{Job Title} building {what you build} with {tech1} and {tech2}' OR '{Job Title} — {brief proof-point or differentiator}'. Keep exact job title from JD. ≤76 chars. Never use 'graduate' or 'new grad' language. Examples: 'Software Engineer building REST APIs in Python and Go with cloud automation' | 'Backend Engineer — 4+ prod features/sprint; FastAPI, distributed systems'

resume_rules.no_fabrication: NEVER add tools, metrics, features, or claims not in this file. Cross-check project stack ground truth before writing bullets.
resume_rules.tagline_format: {Exact Job Title} experienced in Tech1, Tech2, Tech3, and Tech4
resume_rules.action_verb_rule: No two bullets in a single resume should start with the same action verb. Use varied verbs: Built, Designed, Engineered, Developed, Automated, Configured, Implemented, Deployed, Wrote, Contributed, Shipped, Optimized, Integrated, Orchestrated, Scaffolded, Diagnosed, Collaborated, Analyzed.
resume_rules.para_count: Dynamic (v2.3+). Formula: 3+3 + 1+sum(work,1+bullets) + 1+sum(proj,1+bullets) + 1+5. Typical: 2-job x 5b + 3-proj x 3b = 38 (1-page). 3-job x 5b + 3-proj x 3b = 44. Check actual output in Word.
resume_rules.format: DOCX via buildv2.js (v2.3+). data.work=[{id,bullets[]}]. Work IDs: gitlab|carboncopies|udayton|augustana.
resume_rules.buzzwords_front: Lead each bullet with the most important/searchable keyword or technology
resume_rules.tools_in_bullets: Every bullet should mention at least one specific tool/tech used
resume_rules.research_bullets: State PURPOSE + ACHIEVEMENT + IMPACT. Mention co-authorship, venue, and result. Never conflate KSE2024 (RL) with Coq paper (formal verification).
resume_rules.security_framing: Frame research as improving software/hardware security and safety through experimentation and verification.
resume_rules.project_github: Name bold, GitHub hyperlink right-aligned before date. Use short_stack (3-4 techs). Format: 'Name | short_stack    GitHub  Date' (all ≤116 chars).
resume_rules.work_line_format: Combined: 'Bold Title | Company — Location    Bold Dates' (single line). Titles from WORK_META in buildv2.js.
resume_rules.3_job_layout: Use gitlab + carboncopies + udayton for most roles. Add augustana for academic/research roles. 3-job + 3-proj x 3b = 44 paras (likely 1-page; verify in Word).

fabrication_warnings.kse2024_is_rl_NOT_coq: KSE 2024 paper = Adversarial RL robustness + entropy-based imposter detection. Viet = 2nd author. NSF-funded. NOT about Coq or formal verification.
fabrication_warnings.coq_paper_is_NOT_published_at_kse: Coq/MEMOCODE paper = Program Graphs to Proofs. Viet = 1st author. DRAFT — not yet published. NOT published at KSE 2024.
fabrication_warnings.mrr_is_python_NOT_csharp: MRR Dashboard = Python/FastAPI/BigQuery. HomeBoard = C#/ASP.NET Core 8.
fabrication_warnings.no_500_code_paths: Never say 'eliminated manual inspection across 500+ code paths' — fabricated metric not from any paper.
fabrication_warnings.no_webassembly: Never mention WebAssembly — not in any verified project or paper.
fabrication_warnings.no_websocket_unless_homelab: WebSocket not used in Carboncopies work — only in homelab monitoring stack.

quality_review._note: Bullets flagged as activity-only or missing business context. Improve when real numbers are available — DO NOT fabricate. Ask Viet for the actual before/after or outcome.

gitlab_collaborated.current: Collaborated with senior engineers on code review, iterating Ruby implementations to meet production standards
gitlab_collaborated.problem: Activity-only — 'to meet production standards' is vague. No outcome.
gitlab_collaborated.improve_to: Collaborated with senior engineers on code review, refactoring Ruby pipeline logic to cut [X] edge-case failures per week
gitlab_collaborated.needs_from_viet: What specific improvement came out of the code review cycles? Bug rate? Performance? Latency?

gitlab_built_git_scripts.current: Built Git scripting workflows for repository automation and CI/CD integration across open-source projects
gitlab_built_git_scripts.problem: No outcome — what time or errors did the automation save?
gitlab_built_git_scripts.improve_to: Built Git automation scripts reducing [X] hours/week of manual repository maintenance across [N] open-source repos
gitlab_built_git_scripts.needs_from_viet: How many repos? Time saved per week?

carb_wrote_runbooks.current: Wrote runbooks for distributed simulation systems; reduced onboarding time and improved cross-team handoffs
carb_wrote_runbooks.problem: Vague — 'reduced onboarding time' by how much? From what to what?
carb_wrote_runbooks.improve_to: Wrote runbooks for 3 distributed simulation systems, cutting new-engineer onboarding from [X days] to [Y hours]
carb_wrote_runbooks.needs_from_viet: Actual before/after onboarding time. Even approximate is fine.

homelab_provisioned.current: Provisioned 15+ VM cluster on Proxmox at 99.5% uptime with VLAN segmentation, dual-NIC routing, and health checks
homelab_provisioned.problem: Missing business context — WHY does this cluster exist? What does it enable?
homelab_provisioned.improve_to: Provisioned 15+ VM homelab at 99.5% uptime; validated infrastructure patterns (Terraform, VLAN, monitoring) later applied to production deployments
homelab_provisioned.needs_from_viet: Confirm: did homelab patterns actually inform production work at Carboncopies or elsewhere?

udra_authored_docs.current: Authored technical documentation for 3 systems; presented research to cross-functional teams of 100+ students
udra_authored_docs.problem: Weak — '100+ students' is the audience size, not the impact. What changed after the presentation?
udra_authored_docs.improve_to: Authored technical documentation for 3 research systems; led cross-team presentations that [informed X decisions / led to Y adoption]
udra_authored_docs.needs_from_viet: What happened as a result of the presentations? Any adoption, follow-up research, citations?

akamai_v23.used: Software Engineer experienced in Python, REST APIs, Go, and Cloud Services
akamai_v23.problem: Generic — 'experienced in Tech1-4' differentiates nothing. Every candidate says this.
akamai_v23.better: Software Engineer building REST APIs in Python and Go with cloud automation
akamai_v23.or: Software Engineer — FastAPI, Terraform/Ansible, open-source contributor (GitLab CE)
